curator: auto (generate_day.py — reals verified verbatim vs source + cross-validated)

quotes.text: You ever actually READ the back of a shampoo bottle? 'Lather, rinse, REPEAT.' Repeat?! How many times, you sadistic bastards?! There's no exit clause! I've been in this shower since the NINETIES! My fingers are RAISINS! Somebody has to tell these animals when to STOP!
quotes.speaker: Gilbert Gottfried
quotes.context: Riffing on everyday absurdities, a 2009 stand-up set
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the shrieking escalation over a tiny everyday thing is peak Gottfried. The tell: the real Gilbert would've made it filthier and more uncomfortable; this one stays oddly PG for him.

quotes.text: My shrink told me I have an intimacy problem. I said how would you know, you barely know me. Then we both sat there for forty-five minutes not making eye contact, and honestly? Best damn date I've had in years.
quotes.speaker: Garry Shandling
quotes.context: A 1994 late-night appearance, Larry Sanders era
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the self-deprecating anxiety-as-comedy and the meta-relationship beat are textbook Shandling. The tell: his real bits usually turned the knife on himself one more time; this one lets him off easy.

quotes.text: If you're gonna do it, you might as well fuckin' put your name above the door. It's always been about me anyway.
quotes.speaker: Liam Gallagher
quotes.context: Oasis frontman, on going solo and releasing music under his own name rather than a band name, in his trademark blunt interview style (Vice/Noisey interview).

quotes.text: I once asked a Jesuit priest what was the best short prayer he knew. He said, "Fuck it," as in "Fuck it; it's in God's hands."
quotes.speaker: Anthony Hopkins
quotes.context: Anthony Hopkins recounting a Jesuit priest's answer when he asked for the best short prayer — an anecdote he has told publicly, including on Inside the Actors Studio.

quotes.text: My brother said be cool, just be cool. So I'm being cool. Meanwhile this dude's got a velvet rope around his REFRIGERATOR. A rope! On the fridge! In his own house! And he's looking at me like I'M the weird one for wanting a damn sandwich. That was the night I learned famous people are out of their minds.
quotes.speaker: Charlie Murphy
quotes.context: Telling a backstage story on a 2010 stand-up special
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the deadpan 'be cool, just be cool' build into an absurd celebrity detail is exactly the Charlie Murphy True-Hollywood-Story rhythm. The tell: the real one would've named the celebrity and escalated three more times.

quotes.text: I bought a smoke detector and the guy asked if I wanted the kind that talks. Talks? I don't need my ceiling negotiating with me at three in the morning. If there's a fire, beep. We are not gonna workshop it. I trust the beep. The beep has never once lied to me.
quotes.speaker: Mitch Hedberg
quotes.context: His deadpan one-liners, a 2003 Comedy Central set
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the over-literal argument with a household object is the Hedberg engine. The tell: a real Hedberg line lands in one breath; this one explains the joke a sentence too far.
